tweets.text:
Please join me in praying for the family of Coast Guard Petty Officer 2nd Class Tyler Jaggers.

Tyler Jaggers, an aviation survival technician assigned to Air Station Astoria, Oregon, passed away yesterday, as a result of critical injuries sustained during a February 27 medical evacuation mission which was offshore from Cape Flattery, Washington.
 
Prior to Tyler Jaggers passing, surrounded by his parents, sister, fiance, friends, and @USCG temamates, Jaggers was meritoriously advanced to Petty Officer Second Class and awarded the Distinguished Flying Cross—one of the highest military awards for heroism during aerial flight. 

We are grateful for his faithful service.

tweets.text:
Thanks to President Trump’s leadership and the dedicated work of DHS personnel, our department has achieved historic results and made communities safer.

Border apprehensions are at the lowest level since tracking began in 1992. Nearly 3 million illegal aliens have left the United States. ICE has arrested over 1,500 Known or Suspected Terrorists (KSTs) and more than 7,700 gang members. Fentanyl trafficking at the southern border has dropped by more than half compared to the same period in 2024. Of the more than 450,000 unaccompanied alien children lost under the Biden administration, 145,000 have been located under President Trump—and we will not stop until every last one is found. These are just some of the many successes of DHS.

The shutdown is placing tremendous strain on the men and women of TSA, the Secret Service, and the Coast Guard. The Department must be reopened and fully funded to ensure our homeland remains prepared to respond to potential threats or retaliatory measures.

author.description: Secretary of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security under the leadership of @POTUS Trump

tweets.text:
Today marks the Department of Homeland Security’s 23rd anniversary. Less than two years after the devastating attacks of September 11, 2001, DHS was established. Since then, DHS has stood at the helm of our country’s national security, protecting the American people and our homeland.

I am especially grateful to those of you who are serving during the current shutdown without a paycheck, and I thank you for your continued service and sacrifice to our nation.

As threats evolve, DHS is there. By air, land, sea, or cyberspace, the DHS workforce boldly confronts national threats every day. The work of DHS is not always visible, but we are working diligently to prevent cybersecurity and terrorism threats, safeguard the southern border, reform our broken immigration system, and help Americans in the wake of natural disasters.

I would also like to honor the colleagues we have lost over the last 23 years in the line of duty. Their service and memory strengthen our resolve.

Together, we will ensure that the United States remains a beacon of freedom, safety, and security for generations to come.

May God richly bless you all.
